title: Pre/post comprehension-check instrument + pilot on a reader group
context: The M0 success metric requires measurable comprehension improvement on >= 3 core misconceptions in a pilot cohort; M1 requires comprehension lift measured on >= 3 core misconceptions. Without a validated pre/post instrument, there is no evidence that the explainers actually correct the target misreadings — and 'written' without 'comprehension improved' fails the Elyos 'delivered, not merged' bar. This task designs the comprehension-check questions (covering at minimum HR, KM/censoring, and CI-vs-p-value misconceptions), pilots it on a real reader group (not self-reported), and records the results. This task depends on the M0 pilot explainers (hr-004, km-006) being available for readers.
objective: Design a short pre/post misconception quiz covering at least the HR, Kaplan-Meier, and confidence-interval/p-value misconceptions, pilot it on >= 1 real reader group before and after reading the explainers, and record the comprehension change as a project outcome metric.
acceptanceCriteria: A comprehension-check instrument is designed with >= 5 items covering at minimum three target misconceptions: (1) HR as a simple risk ratio or constant risk reduction, (2) reading KM curve tails without regard to at-risk numbers, (3) non-significant p-value as 'no effect' or significant as 'important/large effect'.

Each item is a scenario-based question (not a definition quiz) testing applied misinterpretation recognition; each item has a keyed correct answer and is reviewed by the biostatistician reviewer (reviewer-001) for correctness.

The instrument is piloted in pre/post format on >= 1 real reader group (not self-reported use); the group is identified and their participation is documented (role type: e.g. 'medical students', 'oncology fellows', 'journal club participants'; not names).
Pre and post scores are recorded and the difference per item is computed and committed; the results report states whether measurable improvement occurred on each of the >= 3 target misconceptions.
The instrument and results are committed to the project repo; the biostatistician reviewer has approved the instrument items as statistically correct before the pilot.
If measurable improvement is not observed on a misconception, that negative result is recorded honestly and the relevant explainer is flagged for revision.
The results are linked from the outcomes tracking document and contribute to the M0 definition of done ('comprehension check piloted on >= 1 reader group').
resources: PLAN.md — Success metrics (comprehension lift), M0 exit criteria
Literature on medical education pre/post misconception instruments; CONSORT reporting for educational interventions
output: A committed comprehension-check instrument (>= 5 items, biostatistician-approved) and a pilot results document recording pre/post scores on >= 1 real reader group, with comprehension change reported per misconception. Feeds the M0 definition of done.
